heading: Appointments to positions classified above GS–15
label: 3324 Appointments to positions classified above GS–15
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1966ActDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 1105(i), “(i)”, .June 20, 1958Pub. L. 85–462, § 1072 Stat. 213, , .Sept. 13, 1960Pub. L. 86–76874 Stat. 910In subsection (a), the words “in GS–16, 17, and 18” are substituted for “in grades 16, 17, and 18 of the General Schedule”.In subsection (a)(2), the words “by the President” are coextensive with and substituted for “by the President alone or by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate”.In subsection (a)(4)(A), the words “Office of Emergency Planning” are substituted for “Office of Defense Mobilization” on authority of 1958 Reorg. Plan No. 1, § 2(a), effective , , as amended , , , and , , . Reference to “President’s Advisory Committee on Government Organization” is omitted since the Committee was abolished by Executive Order No. 10917, .July 1, 1958Aug. 26, 1958Sept. 22, 1961February 10, 196172 Stat. 1799Pub. L. 85–76372 Stat. 861Pub. L. 87–29675 Stat. 630In subsection (a)(4)(B), the words “ ‘Emergency Fund for the President’ by the Treasury, Post Office, and Executive Office Appropriation Act, 1966” are substituted for “ ‘Emergency Fund for the President, National Defense’ by the General Government Matters Appropriation Act, 1959” to reflect the heading and title of the current appropriation Act.Subsection (b) is added on authority of former sections 1072 and 1072a, which are carried into section 5115.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.
1967ActThis section amends to correct typographical errors.5 U.S.C. 3324(a)(4)(A)

References in TextThe Treasury, Post Office, and Executive Office Appropriation Act, 1966, referred to in subsec. (a)(4)(B), is , , . For classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 89–5779 Stat. 196June 30, 1965

Amendments2008—Subsec. (a). substituted “the Director of the Office of Personnel Management on the basis of qualification standards developed by the agency involved in accordance with criteria specified in regulations prescribed by the Director” for “the Office of Personnel Management” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 110–3721992—, substituted “GS–15” for “GA–15” in section catchline.Pub. L. 102–378, § 2(11)(A)Subsec. (a)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) read as follows: “provided for in ;”.Pub. L. 102–378, § 2(11)(B)section 5108(c)(2) of this title1990—, which directed that “to positions classified above GA–15” be substituted for “at GS–16, 17, or 18” in section catchline, was executed by making the substitution for “at GS–16, 17, and 18”, as the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 101–509, § 529 [title I, § 101(b)(9)(C)(ii)]Subsec. (a). , substituted “classified above GS–15 pursuant to section 5108” for “in GS–16, 17, or 18”.Pub. L. 101–509, § 529 [title I, § 101(b)(9)(C)(i)]1979—Subsec. (a)(4)(A). struck out reference to Office of Emergency Planning.Pub. L. 96–541978—Subsecs. (a), (b). substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Office” for “Commission”.Pub. L. 95–454

children.text: to which appointment is made by the Chief Judge of the United States Tax Court;
to which appointment is made by the President;
to which appointment is made by the Librarian of Congress; or

children.label: A
children.text: appropriations for the Executive Office of the President under the heading “The White House Office”, “Special Proj­ects”, “Council of Economic Advisers”, or “National Security Council”; or

children.label: B
children.text: funds appropriated to the President under the heading “Emergency Fund for the President” by the Treasury, Post Office, and Executive Office Appropriation Act, 1966, or a later statute making appropriations for the same purpose.

children.label: b
children.text: The Office may prescribe regulations necessary for the administration of this section.
